title: California Polytechnic State University
text:
Responsibilities
As a teaching assistant, I contributed to the courses by:

Delivering tutorials and computer practical sessions
Creating and revising educational materials for tutorials and computer practical sessions
Holding office hours
Grading homework and lesson plans
Providing personalized feedback for homework, quizzes, exams, and lesson plans
Facilitating outdoor activities
Partnering with NGOs to develop and facilitate community engagement activities

title: Sinem Demirci
text:
At the moment, this page mainly brings together blog posts and short pieces I have written for other websites. My hope, however, is to gradually grow it into a space where I can share my own reflections on teaching, statistics and data science education, systems literacy, academic life, and occasional ideas that don’t quite fit anywhere else.

P.S. If you know me personally, you probably know that I love communicating through humor and an excessive number of references to movies, TV shows, internet videos, and song lyrics. Whether I’ll have the courage to do the same here remains to be seen.




Şimdilik bu sayfada ağırlıklı olarak başka web sitelerinde yer alan blog yazılarını bulabilirsiniz. Ancak zamanla burayı; öğretim, istatistik ve veri bilimi eğitimi, sistem okuryazarlığı, akademik yaşam ve bazen de başka hiçbir yere sığmayan düşüncelerimi paylaştığım bir alana dönüştürmeyi umuyorum.

Not. Beni yakından tanıyorsanız, iletişim kurarken çoğunlukla (yer yer ofansif) mizaha başvurduğumu ve film, dizi, internet videosu ile şarkı sözlerine bitmek bilmeyen göndermeler yapmayı sevdiğimi biliyorsunuzdur. Bunu burada da yapacak cesareti bulabilecek miyim, hep birlikte göreceğiz.







Does current curricula hinder student understanding of complex global water systems? 




Öğretmenlerin dijital yeterlikleri [Teacher Digital Competencies]. 




COVID-19 ve dünyada okulların durumu [COVID-19 and The Status of the Schools Worldwide].

title: Sinem Demirci
text:
At the moment, this page mainly brings together blog posts and short pieces I have written for other websites. My hope, however, is to gradually grow it into a space where I can share my own reflections on teaching, statistics and data science education, systems literacy, academic life, and occasional ideas that don’t quite fit anywhere else.

P.S. If you know me personally, you probably know that I love communicating through humor and an excessive number of references to movies, TV shows, internet videos, and song lyrics. Whether I’ll have the courage to do the same here remains to be seen.

title: Sinem Demirci
text:
Bio
I am a Lecturer in the Statistics Department at Cal Poly. My interdisciplinary research spans Statistics & Data Science Education and Environmental & Sustainability Education.
I am a quantitative researcher in education and a qualitative researcher in statistics and data science education. Yes, you read that correctly. I use quantitative research methodologies to analyze large educational datasets and investigate teaching and learning at scale. At the same time, my work in Statistics and Data Science Education primarily employs qualitative research methodologies to better understand instructional practices, curriculum development, and students’ learning experiences.
My research in Environmental and Sustainability Education exists in a parallel research universe. I love to read, write, and think about how people make sense of complex adaptive systems, what current educational approaches overlook, and how we might design learning experiences that cultivate systems literacy to live in a better planet.
